volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Merchant (Section 4.4.750 - Section 4.5.812)

sections.label: Section 4.4.750
sections.translation: Are you not going to have a dinner ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.750b
sections.translation: We are full already .

sections.label: Section 4.4.751
sections.translation: But —

sections.label: Section 4.4.751b
sections.translation: I ’ m utterly undone .

sections.label: Section 4.4.751c
sections.original: Quid ais tu ? etiamne haec illi tibi
sections.translation: What say you ? Have those persons ordered these things to be brought to you as well , between whom you were appointed arbitrator ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.753
sections.original: Haecin tua est amica , quam dudum mihi
sections.translation: Is this person ( pointing to DORIPPA ) your mistress , whom a little time since you told me you were in love with , when you were buying the provisions ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.754b
sections.translation: Won ’ t you hold your tongue ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.755
sections.original: Satis scítum filum mulieris . verum hercle anet .
sections.translation: A very pretty figure of a woman ! I ’ faith , she does love a sweetheart .

sections.label: Section 4.4.756
sections.translation: Won ’ t you be off to perdition ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.756b
sections.translation: She ’ s not amiss .

sections.label: Section 4.4.756c
sections.translation: But you are amiss .

sections.label: Section 4.4.757
sections.translation: I ’ troth , I do fancy she ’ s a nice bed-fellow .

sections.label: Section 4.4.757b
sections.translation: Won ’ t you be off ? I ’ m not the person that hired you just now .

sections.label: Section 4.4.758b
sections.translation: How ’ s that ? Nay but , upon my faith , you are that very man .

sections.label: Section 4.4.759b
sections.translation: Alas wretch that I am !

sections.label: Section 4.4.760
sections.original: Nempe uxor rurist tua , quam dudum dixeras
sections.translation: Your wife ’ s in the country , I suppose , whom you were saying a little time ago you hated full as much as vipers .

sections.label: Section 4.4.761b
sections.translation: I , said that to you ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.762
sections.translation: Aye , to me , upon my faith .

sections.label: Section 4.4.762b
sections.translation: So may Jupiter love me , wife , I never did say that .

sections.label: Section 4.4.763b
sections.translation: Do you deny that as well ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.764
sections.original: palam istaec fiunt , te me odisse .
sections.translation: This is made clear , that you detest me .

sections.label: Section 4.4.764b
sections.translation: But I deny it .

sections.label: Section 4.4.765
sections.original: Non , non te odisse aiebat , sed uxorem suam ;
sections.translation: He didn ’ t say he hated you , but his wife .

sections.label: Section 4.4.766
sections.original: et uxórem suam ruri esse aiebat .
sections.translation: And he said that his wife was in the country .

sections.label: Section 4.4.766b
sections.translation: This is she . Why are you annoying me ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.767b
sections.translation: Because you say that you don ’ t know me . Are you afraid of her ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.768b
sections.translation: I ’ m wise in being so ; for she ’ s my only companion .

sections.label: Section 4.4.769
sections.translation: Do you wish to use my services ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.769b
sections.translation: I don ’ t wish .

sections.label: Section 4.4.769c
sections.translation: Give me my pay .

sections.label: Section 4.4.770
sections.translation: Ask for it to-morrow ; it shall be given you ; for the present , be off .

sections.label: Section 4.4.770b
sections.translation: ( Aside .) Alas , wretch that I am !

sections.label: Section 4.4.771
sections.original: Nunc ego verum illud verbum esse experior vetus :
sections.translation: I now find that that old saying is a true one , that some bad comes through a bad neighbour .

sections.label: Section 4.4.773
sections.original: Cur hic astamus ? quin abimus ? incommodi
sections.translation: Why are we standing here ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.774
sections.original: si quid tibi evénit , id non est culpa mea .
sections.translation: If any inconvenience happens to you , that ’ s not my fault .

sections.label: Section 4.4.775
sections.translation: Why , you are utterly ruining wretched me

sections.label: Section 4.4.775b
sections.translation: I understand now what you want . You mean , you wish me to go away from here .

sections.label: Section 4.4.776b
sections.translation: I do wish it , I say .

sections.label: Section 4.4.776c
sections.translation: I ’ ll be off . Pay me a drachma .

sections.label: Section 4.4.777b
sections.translation: It shall be paid .

sections.label: Section 4.4.777c
sections.translation: Then order it to be paid me , please . It can be paid in the meantime , while they are putting down the provisions .

sections.label: Section 4.4.778b
sections.translation: Why don ’ t you be off ? Can ’ t you cease being troublesome ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.779b
sections.translation: Come , do you set down

sections.label: Section 4.4.780
sections.original: obsonium istuc ante pedes illi seni .
sections.translation: those provisions before the feet of that old gentleman . These baskets I ’ ll order to be fetched from your house either by-and-by or else to-morrow . ( To the SCULLIONS .) Do you follow me .

sections.label: Section 4.4.782b
sections.translation: Perhaps you are surprised at that Cook , that he came and brought these things . I ’ ll tell you why it is .

sections.label: Section 4.4.784
sections.original: Non miror si quid damni facis aut flagiti .
sections.translation: I ’ m not surprised if you do anything wrongful or criminal ;

sections.label: Section 4.4.785
sections.original: nec pol ego patiar , sic me nuptam tam male
sections.translation: and , by heavens , I ’ ll not put up with it , that I am married thus unfortunately , and that harlots are brought into my house in this way . Syra , go ask my father , in my name , to come here directly together with you .

sections.label: Section 4.4.788b
sections.translation: I ’ ll go .

sections.label: Section 4.4.789
sections.original: Nescis negoti quid sit , uxor , obsecro .
sections.translation: Prithee , wife , you don ’ t know what the matter is .

sections.label: Section 4.4.790
sections.translation: In set form now will I make oath , that I have never had anything to do with her . ( Exit SYRA .) What , is Syra gone now ? By heaven , I ’ m undone ! ( DORIPPA goes into the house .) ( to himself .) But , see , she ’ s off as well ! Woe to wretched me ! Then , neighbour Demipho , may the Gods and Goddesses confound you , together with your mistress and your intriguings !

sections.label: Section 4.4.795
sections.translation: He has most unjustly loaded me with suspicions ; he has stirred up enemies against me . At home my wife is most infuriated . I ’ ll be off to the Forum , and tell this to Demipho , that I ’ ll drag this woman by the hair into the street , unless he takes her hence out of this house wherever he chooses .

sections.label: Section 4.4.800
sections.original: uxor , heus uxor , quamquam tu irata es mihi ,
sections.translation: ( Goes to the door and calls .) Hark you ! wife , wife ! although you ’ re angry at me , you ’ ll order , if you are wise , these things to be carried hence indoors . We shall be able by-and-by to dine all the better upon the same .

sections.label: Section 4.5.803
sections.original: Era quo me misit , ad patrem , non est domi :
sections.translation: Whither my mistress sent me , to her father —, he ’ s not at home ; they said that he has gone off into the country . Now , I ’ ll take home this answer .

sections.label: Section 4.5.805
sections.translation: I ’ m quite tired with hunting the whole city through ; I find nothing whatever about this woman . But my mother has returned from the country ; for I see Syra standing before the house . Syra !

sections.label: Section 4.5.808b
sections.translation: Who is it that ’ s calling me ?

sections.label: Section 4.5.809
sections.translation: ’ Tis I , your master and foster-child .

sections.label: Section 4.5.809b
sections.translation: Save you , my foster-child .

sections.label: Section 4.5.810
sections.original: Iam mater rure rediit ? responde mihi .
sections.translation: Has my mother returned from the country then ? Answer me .

sections.label: Section 4.5.811
sections.original: Sua quidem salute ac familiai maxuma .
sections.translation: Aye , for her own especial sake and that of the family .

sections.label: Section 4.5.812
sections.translation: What is it that ’ s the matter ?
